description_long: This is a metallic fork with a simple, utilitarian design, featuring a straight handle that tapers slightly towards the head. The fork has four evenly spaced tines that are slightly curved upward, ideal for picking up food. The handle is rectangular in cross-section and appears to have a smooth, unadorned surface with no visible decorative elements. The entire object has a uniform metallic gray color, suggesting it is made from stainless steel or a similar metal. The fork's surface is smooth and slightly reflective, indicating a polished finish. There are no visible logos, engravings, or markings on the handle or tines.
description: A plain, metallic fork with four curved tines and a straight, smooth handle, finished in polished gray metal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four evenly spaced tines and the straight, tapering handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gentle upward curve of the tines and the flat, rectangular handle.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full width of the tines and the handle's rectangular cross-section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same gentle curve of the tines and the handle's flat profile.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is straight, rectangular in cross-section, and tapers slightly toward the tines with a smooth, undecorated surface.
attribute_tine_alignment: The tines are parallel and evenly spaced, with a slight upward curve and no visible irregularities.
